client.name: The Pallet Network
client.business_model: Federated network of ~120 member depots feeding a central hub at Swadlincote

title: Process Flow with Shipsy

last_mile.tracing: Tracing details captured in Shipsy sent back to TPN Connect

learnings: TPN runs at 60% capacity; trucks wait ~4 hours at hub due to manual booking and scheduling
12 staging areas at Swadlincote with 20-30% sorting delays caused by paper-based manifests
Member depots use manual pallet scanning, no real-time tracking or partner-level visibility
Legacy on-prem TPN Connect — no cloud APIs, no integration with modern shipper systems
Manual postcode-based delivery assignment with 20:00 planning starts and repeated edits
Revenue leakage from manual rate-card reconciliation and partner payout misalignment
No alerts on missed NI/ROI ferry connections or misrouted priority pallets
summary: Customer portal for pallet booking & label printing (single + bulk + ad-hoc)
Real-time IoT trunk tracking across depots and Swadlincote hub
Hub Ops app with guided loading, dock assignment, and pallet verification
Driver app with cluster sequencing, ePOC/ePOD, and dynamic resequencing
Automated rate-card invoicing on sell- and buy-side with reconciliation
BI dashboards for partner performance, hub throughput, and SLA adherence
ai_impact: AI address quality enhancement → fewer failed deliveries on the last mile
Auto-allocation routing engine with 200+ constraints replaces manual postcode planning
Control Tower agent flags missed scans, ferry cutoffs, and SLA breaches in real time
Driver gamification scores + leaderboards drive on-time and POD-quality behaviours
AI dispute resolution shrinks claim cycle times across shippers and member depots
Settlement agent reconciles invoices vs computed charges and surfaces discrepancies
